steps.title: Identify the structures around the blank
steps.content: The sentence has a main independent clause ending at the blank: “The Suzuki method is an approach to music instruction.” After the blank comes an infinitive phrase “to teach music in the same natural way children learn language” and then a finite verb “emphasizes immersion in a musical community.” The blank must introduce a relative clause that incorporates both elements.

steps.title: Apply the rule for relative clauses with inserted description
steps.content:
When a relative clause includes added description, that description must be set off by commas as a parenthetical detail (phrased as “aiming to...” rather than a full verb), so the relative clause maintains one clear main verb. Choice B correctly uses this structure: “that” beginning the clause, followed by the parenthetical description in commas, then “emphasizes” as the main verb. Choices A, C, and D either awkwardly repeat verbs (A) or create a comma splice by attempting two main verbs in the clause (C and D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
